title: Config files included in declaration emit pollutes dist output
reasoning: The include array contains "*.config.ts" which will match vitest.config.ts, bundler configs, etc. Combined with declaration: true and outDir: ./dist, running tsc will emit .d.ts and .js files for these development-only config files into the dist/ directory. If dist/ is published as a package, these artifacts are unnecessary noise and could confuse consumers.
reproduction: Run `bunx tsc --noEmit false` and inspect dist/ for config file outputs (e.g., dist/vitest.config.d.ts).
recommendation: Either exclude *.config.ts from the main tsconfig and use a separate tsconfig for type-checking configs, or add "exclude": ["*.config.ts"] and rely on the editor/vitest for config file type-checking. Alternatively, set noEmit for a type-check-only config and use a separate tsconfig.build.json without config files for emit.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature, and build output hygiene is typically not covered by unit tests.
suggestedRegressionTest: A CI step that runs tsc and asserts no *.config.* files appear in dist/ output.
minimumFixScope: Remove "*.config.ts" from the include array or add it to exclude, and use a separate tsconfig for editor type-checking of config files.
